objective: Unblock the V3.36 pilot safely by adding credential hygiene, local-secret templates, readiness checks, and exact execution instructions without storing or printing real API secrets.
forbidden: Do not output real API tokens, passwords, cookies, or browser session values.
Do not commit or report configs/data_credentials.json contents.
Do not treat Chrome login state as an API credential.
Do not run model, factor, or portfolio backtests.
acceptance_criteria: A local credential template exists with placeholders only.
Readiness checks distinguish SDK availability from credential availability.
Execution instructions are exact but do not contain secrets.
If credentials are still absent, task remains blocked at acquisition level with self-check passing.
failure_conditions: Any output contains a real-looking token or password.
configs/data_credentials.json is created with placeholder or real values by this task.
Pilot acquisition is run without explicit execution and network flags.
Any blocked dataset is marked acquired.
quality_gates: credential values are masked and never printed.
execution remains blocked until credentials are present.
next_handoff: After the user supplies credentials locally, rerun V3.36 with explicit pilot execution enabled; only then can data_steward validate acquired pilot data.
